narrative: FY2023-24 gives a clearer standalone comparison. Jindal Stainless leads on total POSH complaints with 0 and also ties with SAIL for best performance on upheld complaints with 0. JSW follows with 1 total complaint and 1 upheld complaint. SAIL reported 3 total and 0 upheld, improving materially from its FY2022-23 reported level. Tata remains the laggard on both metrics, with 20 total complaints and 8 upheld in the standardised table. Tata’s own FY2023-24 BRSR shows a strong process architecture: written POSH complaints to the Internal Committee, confidential handling, investigation including evidence and witness review, non-victimisation safeguards, awareness and training, and whistle-blower protection. This indicates that Tata’s weaker raw ranking may partly reflect higher reporting maturity. Still, the upheld count suggests there were substantiated behavioural failures requiring prevention-oriented action, not just disclosure-system interpretation.
